feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: Trump announces Iran peace deal could be signed on Sunday
entry.content: Ahead of the G7 summit in Évian, Trump announced a US-Iran peace deal could be signed Sunday—reopening the Strait of Hormuz—though Iran disputed the timeline.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 13th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 13th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: 'Take me to America': Bosnia unites behind viral anthem in its World Cup return
entry.content: As the Western Balkan country celebrates its second-ever appearance at the tournament despite its underdog status, a 15-year-old song about life abroad by a much-beloved band has become an unofficial anthem for Bosnian fans, many of whom live abroad as part of the country's large diaspora.

entry.title: Thousands gather in London to celebrate Trooping the Colour
entry.content: Thousands gathered in London for King Charles III's Trooping the Colour birthday parade while anti-monarchy protesters held up pictures of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

entry.title: 'Wake-up call': Europe reacts to Anthropic halting access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models
entry.content: Anthropic said it believed the US government had become aware of a potential means of jailbreaking Fable 5.

entry.title: Brazil v Morocco: where is the under-16s curfew enforced in France?
entry.content: In some municipalities, unprecedented measures target unaccompanied teenagers, following repeated disturbances at festive events.

entry.title: Iran: officials announce dates for Ali Khamenei funeral and burial
entry.content: State media in Iran reported on Saturday, June 13, that the funeral and burial of Ali Khamenei, former leader of the Islamic Republic, will be held from July 4 to July 9, 2026

entry.title: Humans remains found in Polish village, estimation over 30 bodies
entry.content: A macabre discovery was made in Lutoryż near Rzeszów, where renovation work on a plot uncovered human remains identified as foetuses. The land had previously belonged to a 57-year-old pathologist, who has been detained by the authorities.

entry.title: Ukraine and Russia trade strikes as Kyiv inches closer to EU membership
entry.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Wednesday that 11 regions of Ukraine had come under Russian attack since the start of the week.

entry.title: EU migration pact: what it means for Poland and Europe
entry.content: The migration pact is now in force. All EU states must apply solidarity when taking in migrants. We explain what this means for Poland and debunk myths.

entry.title: Swiss voters set to vote on capping population at 10 million
entry.content: Swiss voters will decide Sunday on an SVP-backed cap on population growth, which polls suggest will narrowly fail amid warnings it would harm the economy and EU relations.

entry.title: The passion and resurrection of Kaja Kallas
entry.content: In her weekly newsletter, Euronews’ Europe editor Maria Tadeo reports on the week from hell for the EU’s top diplomat and her plan to salvage the European diplomatic service, plus Pope Leo’s apostolic journey in Spain and the Eurogroup meeting in Luxembourg.

entry.title: Russia's opposition looks for a leader as new party launches in Berlin
entry.content: Around 71% of Russians approve of President Vladimir Putin, according to the state-run pollster VCIOM. Yet a newly founded opposition party in exile believes it can help shape Russia's political future. How realistic are its ambitions?

entry.title: Too much ‘football fever’? Why some World Cup fans should limit their excitement
entry.content: Watching your team play can send your heart racing as fast as if you were sprinting, and for some fans that carries genuine medical risks.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 13th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 13th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Why Anthropic is halting access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models
entry.content: The US government issued a directive ordering Anthropic to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals.

entry.title: Theft of rare Pushkin editions in France: up to 7 years in prison for perpetrators
entry.content: According to French magistrates, the flights may be intended to repatriate Russian cultural heritage, against a backdrop of heightened tensions between Moscow and the West since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

entry.title: Twitter 'miracle' during Pope's visit to Barcelona
entry.content: A photograph of Leon XIV holding the hands of a seven-year-old boy during his visit to Barcelona went viral. Days later, the boy’s parents, convinced they had experienced a miracle, managed to find the photographer thanks to X.com, ChatGPT and Instagram.

entry.title: UK: Royal Opera Chorus marks 2026 World Cup start with London performance
entry.content: London marked the 2026 World Cup kick-off with SeokJong Baek and the Royal Opera Chorus performing Puccini’s ‘Nessun dorma’ in Covent Garden.

entry.title: Could drones target Germany's parliament? Minister warns of growing threat
entry.content: As drone-related security concerns rise across Europe, German Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt is calling for permanent protection of the Bundestag and other constitutional bodies while authorities expand the country's counter-drone capabilities.

entry.title: USA make impressive start to World Cup campaign in front of Hollywood stars
entry.content: Some of Hollywood's biggest names were in attendance for the match at LA's SoFi stadium, including Tom Cruise and Leonardo DiCaprio.

entry.title: Backlash against Kallas reveals hard truths of EU foreign policy
entry.content: The growing criticism of Kaja Kallas as High Representative has laid bare structural shortcomings and tensions in the European Union's foreign policy.

entry.title: Trump says US forces killed leader of the Tren de Aragua gang
entry.content: The Tren de Aragua gang has been designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the US since February 2025.

entry.title: She shoots, she scores: Spain's pioneering women's footballers get big screen treatment
entry.content: Decades before Spain won the World Cup, a group of women toured the country promoting women's football and even played Italy and Portugal with an unofficial national side.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 13th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 13th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: World's first large-scale hydrogen engine starts generating electricity
entry.content: A giant hydrogen-powered engine has successfully supplied electricity to Spain's national grid in what its manufacturer says is a world first for large-scale power generation. Supporters say the technology could help balance renewable energy systems without carbon emissions.

entry.title: David Beckham receives star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
entry.content: Football icon David Beckham was honoured with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, recognising his sporting achievements and influence on the growth of football in the United States.

entry.title: Cuban president announces economic reforms to 'liberalise' the embargo-hit and blockaded island
entry.content: Díaz-Canel announced a slew of reforms in key sectors such as tourism, foreign trade and private business, amid new US sanctions designed to cripple the island nation into submission.

entry.title: European Commission seeks further answers over Russia's participation at the Venice Biennale
entry.content: The European Commission has requested further clarifications from the Venice Biennale Foundation over Russia's participation, saying issues raised earlier remain unresolved and giving it 30 days to respond.

entry.title: Ship from Colombia laden with food and other goods docks in Cuba to help ease crises
entry.content: Regular power outages have intensified since US President Donald Trump threatened tariffs in late January on any country that sells or provides oil to Cuba.

entry.title: Federal judge rejects bid to halt removal of Trump's name from Kennedy Center building
entry.content: Since returning to the White House, Trump has taken repeated measures to put his name and image in official spaces, an abrupt break with American political tradition.

entry.title: Pope returns to Rome on plane offered by Spanish king after technical glitch, Vatican says
entry.content: It was the first time in decades that a papal flight had experienced a problem so serious that it required the pope to change planes.

entry.title: Iran says deal with US closer than ever as Trump warns Tehran to get act together
entry.content: A ceasefire took effect in April, but sporadic episodes of violence have occurred, each time sparking new fears of a return to all-out war, despite Trump repeatedly stating a deal was within reach.

entry.title: EU countries reach deal to unblock accession talks with Ukraine and Moldova
entry.content: Friday's agreement allows Ukraine and Moldova to formally open the first cluster of accession negotiations, known as "Fundamentals".

entry.title: 'Hussarz' roar over Poland as first F-35s officially enter service
entry.content: Residents of several Polish cities heard the roar of F-35 fighter engines as the first three jets delivered to Poland staged a ceremonial flypast over Gdańsk, Warsaw, Kraków and Łódź.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 12th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 12th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: New France-Ireland ferry revives Boulogne's international links
entry.content: A new overnight service linking Cork and Boulogne-sur-Mer has begun operations, restoring international passenger ferries to the northern French port for the first time in 15 years and offering travellers another route that avoids the UK.

entry.title: Belgian driver convicted of murder over carnival car crash that killed seven people
entry.content: The trial was heard in a special hall to accommodate nearly 200 civil parties taking part. The civil claimants include some who are disabled for life and the relatives of those who died.

entry.title: From Florence to Crete: 11 European hotels among the world’s best new stays
entry.content: From restored palaces in Rome and Bucharest to countryside retreats in Devon and Crete, travel magazine Afar has unveiled its annual ranking of the world's best new and revamped hotels - with 11 standout properties in Europe making the cut.

entry.title: EMYA 2026: Which museum will be named Europe’s best?
entry.content: Thirty-four nominees are eyeing this year’s European Museum of the Year Award - from Portugal and Estonia to Türkiye and the United Kingdom. Here are some of the museums competing for the right to be called Europe's best museum of 2026.

entry.title: Any change to EU protection scheme for Ukrainians should be 'gradual', UNHCR deputy chief says
entry.content: Kelly Clements tells Euronews that any planned changes to EU protections for Ukrainian refugees should be carried out "very gradually", as some EU capitals push for excluding military-age men from the scheme.

entry.title: Israel: Tel Aviv pride marchers push for LGBTQ+ equality amid tensions and war
entry.content: Thousands of people joined the Tel Aviv Pride parade on 12 June, filling the city's seafront with rainbow flags, music and calls for greater LGBTQ+ rights in a region where equality remains limited.

entry.title: The path to SAFE II: inside the struggle to revamp Europe's defence financing tool
entry.content: EU policymakers are weighing a SAFE II defence financing tool, with Eastern-flank states pushing for grants over loans. But the final SAFE I uptake, the development of the European defence industry, Ukraine's battlefield-driven tech edge and shifting warfare scenarios are shaping what comes next.

entry.title: US withdraws long-range military capabilities from NATO
entry.content: The move comes at a particularly tense moment, with US Donald Trump increasingly angry at NATO allies over their refusal to join his war on Iran.

entry.title: EU energy ministers eye jet fuel reserves as Strait of Hormuz crisis threatens supply
entry.content: With the peak summer season drawing near, EU energy ministers are wary that jet fuel supply may be further strained, even as analysts see "sharp movement" towards greater jet fuel production within European refineries.

entry.title: Washington plans to slash fighter jets and warships to NATO in Europe, US media report
entry.content: The reported cutbacks come as European nations race to bolster their defence capacities since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 sparked fears that Moscow could attack a NATO country.

entry.title: Pope urges 'reciprocal' integration between migrants and host countries
entry.content: During his visit to Tenerife, the pontiff called for integration based on rights and duties, urging migrants to learn the language, obey the law and join in community life.

entry.title: What to know about the EU's new migration and asylum reforms as they come into effect
entry.content: The bloc’s new migration rules take effect on Friday after months of political wrangling. Here are the changes that are now in force.

entry.title: ‘Land of the fulfilled farmers’: Why the grass seems to grow greener in Austria
entry.content: Austria has repeatedly been acknowledged as a success story in the intergenerational renewal of its farming sector. A combination of hard work at family-run farms, strong public support, qualitative professional training and engaged consumers is revitalizing entire rural communities.

entry.title: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths when quake hit Philippines, officials say
entry.content: The numbers of dead and missing were considerably lowered after multiple verifications, the Office of Civil Defence said in a statement.

entry.title: Gaza Families search ruins after Israeli strikes hit homes
entry.content: Palestinians in central Gaza searched through rubble on 12 June after Israeli strikes destroyed homes in Deir al-Balah and the Maghazi refugee camp, leaving dozens of displaced families without shelter and struggling to recover their belongings.

entry.title: How EU countries watered down 'solidarity' plans to relocate asylum seekers
entry.content: A new migration law introducing "mandatory solidarity" has been all but circumvented by the EU's member states, who will relocate fewer than 9,000 asylum seekers this year – even after the annual minimum was set at 30,000.

entry.title: Everything you need to know about the Jared Kushner resort protests in Albania
entry.content: The European Commission has warned Albania that it must act now to avoid jeopardising its bid to join the bloc, which requires it to comply with EU environmental rules.

entry.title: 'Do not ever use my music': Ariana Grande slams the White House over ICE video
entry.content: The White House has repeatedly faced criticism for using popular music in social media posts without the approval of the artists involved. This time, the Trump administration has decided to hop on a trending track by Ariana Grande.

entry.title: San Andreas Fault reaches highest stress level in 1,000 years
entry.content: Researchers report that tectonic stress on the major San Andreas and San Jacinto fault systems has climbed to its highest levels in a millennium, and in some sections has already surpassed them.

entry.title: Georgieva: Europeans must invest 'together'
entry.content: European economic conditions "have worsened," International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said, as the IMF is now "downgrading" its growth projections and "uplifting" its inflation forecasts. Georgieva recommends that the EU invest "together" and "wisely".

entry.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO: the capitalist space revolution begins
entry.content: World’s biggest IPO: Elon Musk wants to raise $75bn for SpaceX, making him Earth’s first trillionaire, as Rainer Zitelmann explains in an opinion piece for Euronews how it came about and what happens next.

entry.title: Podcast: The most political World Cup to date?
entry.content: What makes the 2026 World Cup so political? What does the entry of the EU Migration and Asylum Pact mean in reality? The two questions we answer on Brussels, My Love? the podcast.

entry.title: Ukraine's drone strikes are methodically cutting Crimea off from Russia
entry.content: Ukraine has significantly intensified its strikes on military and energy sites in and around Russia-occupied Crimea in what is seen as Kyiv’s deliberate strategy of cutting the annexed Ukrainian peninsula off from Russia.

entry.title: Brussels joins growing list of European cities banning shared e-scooters over safety concerns
entry.content: The Belgian capital will remove rental e-scooters from its streets from January 2027, citing rising accident rates, pavement clutter and their increasing use by organised crime groups. Shared bikes and e-bikes will remain available instead.

entry.title: Viral Dua Lipa wedding photos were AI-generated
entry.content: A series of fake images purporting to show pop star Dua Lipa celebrating her wedding in the Sicilian town of Palermo have fooled users and even some digital media outlets.

entry.title: What if Trump actually seized Kharg Island?
entry.content: Kharg Island is a small coral outcrop off the coast of Iran that handles around 90% of the country's crude oil exports, making it key to Tehran's economy.

entry.title: Gaza residents find brief escape in World Cup opener
entry.content: Palestinian Territories residents gathered in tents, cafes and makeshift community spaces on 11 June to watch the opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, seeking a brief escape from the hardships of war.

entry.title: Self-driving cars to be tested on Portuguese roads from July
entry.content: From July, Portugal will allow autonomous vehicles to be tested on public roads under a new decree laying down strict rules on safety, insurance and oversight.

entry.title: Nobody knew what the ref was saying and the internet loved it. World Cup's first meme is alredy here
entry.content: 'Wait, what?': Players were left speechless as the referee explained the VAR decision in an apparent shaky English after a controversial red card.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 12th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 12th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: British artist David Hockney, whose vibrant pool paintings defined an era, dies aged 88
entry.content: Known for his vibrant paintings of Los Angeles swimming pools, David Hockney became a defining figure in 20th-century art, blending vibrant colour and simplified forms into instantly recognisable works.

entry.title: China claims 'spy sea turtles' are studying its coastline
entry.content: Beijing claims foreign intelligence agencies are attaching tracking and monitoring devices to sea creatures to gather information that could expose weaknesses in China's coastal defences.

entry.title: Russian drone barrage hits Kyiv and Mykolaiv as Ukraine reports major fires
entry.content: Russian drone strikes hit multiple locations across Ukraine overnight into 12 June, damaging sites near Kyiv and in the Mykolaiv region, injuring one person and triggering a major fire, while Ukrainian air defences said they intercepted most of the 117 drones launched.

entry.title: NATO to cut troop numbers in Kosovo amid 'improved security situation'
entry.content: The cuts are to be carried out "gradually and in line with conditions on the ground," NATO said.

entry.title: Regulators investigate Ryanair over controversial family seating fees
entry.content: Europe’s largest low-cost airline is under investigation over its policy requiring families with young children to pay for seat reservations. UK regulators will examine whether the fees are unfair and whether they breach rules designed to prevent hidden charges.

entry.title: Mexico's 'missing persons' crisis takes centre stage at the World Cup
entry.content: The discovery of suspected cartel killing sites and a record number of disappearances has renewed scrutiny of one of Mexico's deepest social wounds.

entry.title: ‘Human dignity’ and ‘international law’ sit at the core of the EU's migration overhaul, Brunner says
entry.content: Exclusive: The European Union's new migration and asylum pact takes effect on Friday, and in the run up to the overhaul, the EU’s migration chief Magnus Brunner said he spoke about migration to Pope Leo, a defender of liberal asylum policies.

entry.title: Belgium: Jordan Bardella strengthens ties with Vlaams Belang in Brussels visit
entry.content: Brussels became the latest stop in Jordan Bardella’s European outreach campaign on 11 June. The French far-right leader promoted closer cooperation on migration policy, while facing protests in the Belgian capital.

entry.title: Watch fan reactions as Mexico beat South Africa 2–0 in the World Cup opener
entry.content: Mexico launched its World Cup campaign with a 2-0 victory over South Africa on 11 June. The result triggered celebrations across Mexico City while South African supporters voiced concern over their team's performance and tactics.

entry.title: Did Shakira use a body double at the 2026 World Cup opening ceremony?
entry.content: The internet is convinced Shakira was replaced by a body double at the 2026 World Cup. We review the footage to see how much truth there is to it.

entry.title: Mexico: Tight security and protests surround 2026 World Cup opener
entry.content: Protesters gathered near Mexico City's World Cup opener on 11 June, while thousands of fans headed to the stadium. Brief clashes broke out as demonstrators approached security cordons around the stadium.

entry.title: Newsletter: EU’s historic migration overhaul enters into force
entry.content: In this newsletter: Exclusive interviews with IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva and the European Commissioner for Migration Magnus Brunner.

entry.title: Albania: thousands rally on 12th day of protests against Trump-linked resort
entry.content: Thousands of protesters gathered in Tirana on 11 June for a twelfth consecutive day of demonstrations against a proposed luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, warning that the project threatens Albania’s protected Vjosa-Narta wetlands.

entry.title: Israeli air campaign intensifies across southern Lebanon
entry.content: An early morning strike in Nabatiyeh capped a week of escalating hostilities that saw the US and Iran exchange direct fire. While Israel continues to reject Tehran's demands for a linked ceasefire with Hezbollah, the relentless bombardment has left the southern Lebanese border deserted.

entry.title: Journalist gunned down in Mexico as World Cup kicks off
entry.content: Mexico is regarded as one of the most dangerous and deadly countries for working journalists, according to Reporters Without Borders.

entry.title: Which countries have the most strikes in the EU?
entry.content: In the early part of 2026, Europe already became the stage for widespread demonstrations, leaving classrooms empty, flights grounded, and medical appointments missed. So, where have workers raised their voices the loudest?

entry.title: Guess who's become the youngest woman to be inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame?
entry.content: What a week it's been for Taylor Swift who's celebrating, at 36, becoming the youngest woman to be inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame, just days after watching the New York Knicks complete the biggest comeback in NBA Finals history.

entry.title: Princess Bajrakitiyabha the 'pride of Thailand' dies aged 47 after three-year battle with infection
entry.content: A vocal campaigner for protecting women from domestic violence and the rights of the accused, had been suffering with mycoplasma infection since 2022.

entry.title: Europe Today: World Cup fever, new EU migration era and the Pope's message to Spain
entry.content: The biggest-ever World Cup got underway with hosts Mexico beating South Africa and South Korea upsetting the first European team in action. In Europe, the EU's new Migration and Asylum Pact takes effect today, while Pope Leo XIV wraps up his visit to Spain with a special mass in Tenerife.

entry.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 worth the price? OpenAI may soon become cheaper
entry.content: Is Anthropic's Fable 5 worth the price? OpenAI might soon offer a cheaper alternative

entry.title: The city of international solidarity… and kitsch: 48 hours in Skopje, North Macedonia
entry.content: Ahead of Skopje’s stint as the European Capital of Culture in 2028, I headed to the city to learn more about its fascinating history.

entry.title: Mexico fans celebrate World Cup win over South Africa
entry.content: Mexican fans erupted in celebration after the national team defeated South Africa in the opening match of the 2026 World Cup, with supporters gathering in fan zones and outside Mexico City Stadium to mark the victory.

entry.title: Fortaleza and Recife: Brazil looks to Europe
entry.content: With direct flights from several European capitals, the two major north-eastern Brazilian cities offer beaches, nature, culture and history in a region increasingly open to international tourism.

entry.title: 2-0 victory for Mexico in dramatic World Cup opening match against South Africa
entry.content: Julián Quiñones scored the opening goal of the tournament after nine minutes and 35-year-old striker Raúl Jiménez headed in Mexico's second goal in a 2-0 opening game against South Africa that produced three red cards.

entry.title: Mexico's President Sheinbaum joins fans as World Cup gets underway
entry.content: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum joined thousands of supporters at a fan zone in Mexico City's Zocalo Square as Mexico took on South Africa in the opening match of the 2026 World Cup. Fans packed the square to cheer on the host nation.

entry.title: US slaps sanctions on Cuba's oil and gas company, accusing it of weaponising energy
entry.content: Cuba is already struggling under a decades-old embargo and a lack of petroleum as the US keeps pushing for a change in its economic and political model.

entry.title: Shakira lights up the stage as World Cup kicks off in Mexico City
entry.content: The extravaganza hosted by the United States, Mexico and Canada is the biggest World Cup in history, stretching until the final in New Jersey on 19 July.

entry.title: World Cup street art returns to Rio de Janeiro
entry.content: Rio's Tijuca and Saude neighbourhoods paint streets for Brazil's World Cup dream, turning whole blocks into murals in green and yellow.

entry.title: Fans descend on Mexico City Stadium ahead of World Cup kickoff
entry.content: Fans gathered outside Mexico City Stadium and at public viewing areas ahead of the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: God bless America: Ralph Lauren unveils stamp collection for 250th US anniversary
entry.content: Fashion icon Ralph Lauren has picked a collection of 13 images depicting America as stamps to mark the 250th anniversary of the United States.

entry.title: Trump says he is cancelling fresh strikes on Iran and hints at possible peace deal
entry.content: Trump’s social media post comes after the US and Iran traded strikes for a third day, pushing the Middle East closer to the resumption of a full-scale war.

entry.title: Russia 'lost standing’ despite ‘a breather’ from higher oil prices, IMF chief says
entry.content: While Russia has emerged as one of the very few winners from the recent disruptions to the global oil market amid the Iran war, this is not enough to offset the hit of sanctions and isolation, the managing director of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) told Euronews.

entry.title: No option but to talk to Taliban about migrant returns, EU's migration commissoner says
entry.content: EU countries received about a million asylum applications filed by Afghans between 2013-2024, according to the bloc's data agency.

entry.title: Pope Leo XIV casts wreath into sea in memory of migrants lost on Atlantic route
entry.content: Pope Leo XIV threw a floral wreath into the waters off Spain's Canary Islands to honour migrants who died attempting the dangerous Atlantic crossing. The pontiff also condemned indifference to the plight of migrants during his visit.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 11th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 11th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Kallas defends EU foreign service before staff as debate over its future intensifies
entry.content: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas has backed her own diplomatic service as European capitals, led by Paris, eye a possible EEAS overhaul, ranging from stripping the High Representative's powers to expanding them.

entry.title: Three children dead after car ploughs into school cycling group in the Netherlands
entry.content: The accident happened near the village of Vogelwaarde, close to the Belgian border, and the ages of the children involved are not currently known.

entry.title: Senior MEP fears Airbus-Boeing dispute could reignite EU-US tensions
entry.content: German MEP Bernd Lange fears the end of a crucial corporate truce could give the US grounds to breach the Turnberry agreement, which the European Parliament is expected to adopt next week.

entry.title: Cyprus proposes €32.8bn cut to next EU budget as compromise between rival camps
entry.content: Cyprus proposes a 2% cut — worth €32.8bn — to the EU's draft €2 trillion budget for 2028–2034, in a bid to bridge the gap between "frugals" and cohesion-minded member states. The figures, now on the table for the first time, will serve as the starting point for the sensitive negotiations ahead.

entry.title: These underrated natural wonders are the world’s best-kept secrets
entry.content: From Alaska’s glaciers to a remote Scottish island famed for its puffins and dramatic sea cave, new research based on 54,000 reviews reveals the hidden natural wonders travellers say deserve far more attention – and the famous sites that fail to live up to the hype.

entry.title: First group of Nigerians returns home after anti-immigration protests in South Africa
entry.content: Authorities in South Africa said the returnees were found to be in the country illegally, contradicting Nigerian officials who they were fleeing xenophobic attacks.

entry.title: ILA Berlin: Fighter jets still fly but drones dominate the conversation
entry.content: While flight displays and military hardware continue to draw the crowds, drones, autonomous systems and a new generation of defence companies are increasingly shaping the conversation at ILA Berlin.

entry.title: Meloni tells Brussels to scrap 'rebates' for some member states – or Italy will demand them too
entry.content: The Italian Prime Minister has attacked the EU's disbursement to so-called frugal states and warned Brussels that Rome's patience with them is at an end.

entry.title: German, French and UK envoys meet Lavrov's deputy in Moscow amid E3 peace push
entry.content: Earlier this month, Russian President Vladimir Putin rejected Volodymyr Zelenskyy's offer for a face-to-face meeting to end the fighting.

entry.title: Somali referee denied entry to the US set to officiate UEFA Super Cup final
entry.content: The Super Cup final sees the winners of the Champions League face off against the holders of the Europa League. It is set to take place on 12 August at Stadion Salzburg in Austria.

entry.title: 'Genius of the Renaissance': Leonardo da Vinci exhibition opens in Astana
entry.content: An exhibition dedicated to the inventions of Leonardo da Vinci has opened in Astana, offering visitors a chance to explore the ideas of one of history's most influential inventors, artists and scientists.

entry.title: Two killed and several injured in house fire on Spanish island of Mallorca
entry.content: The floor where the fire started was cordoned off, along with the levels directly above and below it, authorities said.

entry.title: China tests AI robot cleaners in homes
entry.content: China is testing AI robot cleaners in Beijing homes, where machines assist human cleaners in early trials focused on data collection and domestic tasks.

entry.title: Naples ring road is Italy's first smart road: reshaping future mobility
entry.content: Naples' ring road has been certified by Italy's transport ministry as the country's first Smart Road. Sensors, traffic monitoring and car-to-road communication herald the mobility of tomorrow.

entry.title: Scientists discover where whales go to die
entry.content: A newly discovered whale graveyard in the southeastern Indian Ocean is providing scientists with a rare glimpse into how marine life can thrive for millions of years at great depths.

entry.title: UK defence secretary resigns with stinging attack on Keir Starmer's military spending plans
entry.content: The Defence Investment Plan has faced repeated delays despite warnings that the UK's armed forces face a major funding gap.

entry.title: US strikes another tanker in Hormuz as it tightens Iranian port blockade
entry.content: CENTCOM disabled another tanker attempting to bypass the Iran blockade in a third strike this week, just hours after three Indian crew members were confirmed dead in Wednesday's strike on the MT Settebello.

entry.title: US will seize and control Iran's Kharg Island and other key oil facilities, Trump says
entry.content: Trump's social media post comes after the US and Iran traded strikes for a third day, pushing the region closer to the resumption of a full-scale war.

entry.title: Four Seasons reveals second ultra-luxury yacht with sprawling suites and one-to-one service
entry.content: Fresh from the launch of its first vessel, Four Seasons has revealed plans for Four Seasons II, a new ultra-luxury yacht set to debut in 2028.

entry.title: From food and drink to seats and wifi, these are the best airlines in Europe
entry.content: The 2026 APEX Best Awards, which are based on passenger ratings, are out now.

entry.title: EU steps in to shield households from energy price spikes ahead of new carbon costs
entry.content: The upcoming carbon costs due to kick in in 2028 will hit road transport and buildings, likely pushing energy prices higher.

entry.title: Here comes the sun: Stunning Amaterasu show celebrates 160 years of Belgium-Japan relations
entry.content: Inspired by the legend of sun goddess Amaterasu, Be-Japon's acclaimed production reimagines the mythological story through colourful kimonos and a fashion-inspired performances on a catwalk.

entry.title: Congo motorcyclists mobilise as Ebola outbreak fuels fear
entry.content: Dozens of motorcycle taxi drivers rode through eastern DR Congo carrying Ebola prevention messages as health officials seek to combat misinformation and scepticism amid a growing outbreak.

entry.title: WHO Europe chief urges fans to enjoy World Cup as US pushes for travel restrictions
entry.content: As Washington pushes other countries to impose travel restrictions on visitors from affected areas, WHO's European chief says the risk of Ebola spreading at the tournament remains minimal.

entry.title: The 2026 World Cup kicks off today - here's everything you need to know
entry.content: According to FIFA, 2.9 billion people worldwide watched the 2022 World Cup in Qatar on linear TV, with 2.7 billion also streaming the tournament.

entry.title: Pope Leo XIV calls for a stronger response to migration crisis during Canary Islands visit
entry.content: Leo XIV visits Gran Canaria to meet migrants and volunteers in Arguineguín before leading a large open-air mass attended by thousands on the island.

entry.title: Eurogroup chief tells Euronews 'fiscal policy must not contradict ECB' effort to tame inflation
entry.content: Pierrakakis tells Euronews fiscal policy must not contradict the ECB's efforts to tame inflation as the central bank prepares to raise interest rates. Rome is calling for more flexibility pegged to rising energy costs as a result of war.

entry.title: Free World Cup tickets: Airbnb is giving away seats for some host city match-day bookings
entry.content: With the largest football event kicking off today, fans in the US, Mexico and Canada will be able to secure their seats without spending a dime by booking stays in one of the 16 host cities.

entry.title: Can France's justice system really review 70,000 child sexual abuse cases in five weeks?
entry.content: The murder of an 11-year-old schoolgirl in France has sparked outrage at the country's judicial system, prompting the justice minister to launch a mass child abuse case review. But experts are split on how feasible the plan is.

entry.title: ChatGPT can now buy things for you after deal with payments giant Visa
entry.content: Visa has embedded its payment network inside ChatGPT, letting the chatbot independently shop and complete purchases on your behalf.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 11th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 11th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Brussels set to unveil plan to lower electricity bills amid energy crisis
entry.content: The upcoming legislation will propose to have electricity taxed more favourably than gas, reform network charges and encourage the mass deployment of digital devices to track energy consumption in real time, according to a document seen by Euronews.

entry.title: World Cup 2026: supercomputer backs Spain, former stars favour Argentina
entry.content: A member of France’s 1998 World Cup-winning team and the Italian defender who scored in the 2006 final shared their expectations with Euronews

entry.title: Fancy owning your own town? Australia’s smallest settlement is on the market for €243,000
entry.content: With just two residents, a four-bed roadhouse and an unofficial mayor’s job description that includes running the post office and pouring pints, the outback town of Cooladdi is searching for new owners willing to embrace an unconventional way of life.

entry.title: Patrick Bruel free but under judicial supervision after indictment over sexual violence
entry.content: The artist is accused by more than twenty women of sexual violence. Indicted in four cases from 2018–2019, he avoided pre-trial detention sought by prosecutors.

entry.title: 'It's up to the host': EU sports chief responds to World Cup visa row after Somali referee barred
entry.content: Reacting to concerns over US travel restrictions affecting World Cup participants, the EU Commissioner for sport told Euronews' flagship morning show Europe Today that it is for the host countries to decide "who has access", while stressing that "football should be accessible to everyone."

entry.title: ‘Save a life, not a bag’: Airlines could lock overhead bins over evacuation safety concerns
entry.content: The IATA has launched a campaign encouraging passengers to leave baggage behind as every second counts during an emergency situation.

entry.title: European Parliament tests shorter debates to avoid empty seats
entry.content: The European Parliament is testing new rules to avoid empty seats during the debates and engage more with the Commissioners. MEPs often miss discussions due to conflicting political activity.

entry.title: After 144 Years, Pope Leo XIV marks the completion of Sagrada Familia's tallest tower in Barcelona
entry.content: The pontiff blessed the new Tower of Jesus Christ at the Sagrada Família in a ceremony attended by around 120,000 people, ending with a light and fireworks show.

entry.title: Baptism of fire: What are the five most stressful countries for beginner drivers in Europe?
entry.content: Italy is by far more stressful to drive in than the UK, Germany, France and Spain, according to a study. But what are the most uncomfortable roads of all across the whole continent?

entry.title: Haiti embraces World Cup dream after 54-year wait
entry.content: Haiti's Ti Mondial 2026 tournament brings together 48 schools to celebrate the World Cup and promote peace, unity and hope.

entry.title: Who is Jared Kushner - the man funding Albania's controversial property deal?
entry.content: The White House envoy has long attracted attention for his links to US politics and his business dealings around the world, but as controversy grows over a property deal in Albania, many are asking: Who is Jared Kushner?

entry.title: Watch: Empty seats and closed borders? FIFA’s high-stakes bet on a divided America
entry.content: While the football elite rub shoulders in Washington, the game is hitting a physical and metaphorical wall at the US border. International teams, referees and everyday fans find it difficult to see the games which start today. What’s going on there?

entry.title: We need to make 'Europe more resilient', Eurogroup chief says
entry.content: Eurogroup President, Kyriakos Pierrakakis, speaks to Euronews and Maria Tadeo.

entry.title: Police fire water cannon at demonstrators as Belfast anti-immigration protests continue
entry.content: Stephen Ogilvie, the victim of the knife attack in Belfast, sustained severe injuries to his eyes as well as slashes to his face and back and remains in hospital.

entry.title: Newsletter: The bloc’s budget gameplay
entry.content: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup officially kicks off in Mexico on Thursday, the European Union is attempting to figure out it's own on-field strategy for combatting finance crunches triggered by energy shocks and shifting geopolitics.

entry.title: Clashes erupt as police use water cannon near Belfast
entry.content: Police used water cannon during a second night of unrest in Northern Ireland after a Belfast stabbing sparked anti-migrant tensions.

entry.title: Spain: Pope Leo XIV blesses new Sagrada Familia tower, now world’s tallest church
entry.content: Pope Leo XIV blesses Sagrada Familia’s new tower in Barcelona, now the world’s tallest church, in a mass marking 100 years since Antoni Gaudí’s death.

entry.title: The EU’s Digital Markets Act: Fines, delayed features and unclear benefits
entry.content: Apple’s Siri AI delay has turned the DMA from a Brussels bubble debate into a consumer issue. The DMA is meant to ensure a fair market, but what are the measurable benefits for consumers and founders?

entry.title: Thousands protest demanding Albania's PM Rama resigns
entry.content: The demonstrations have grown into a broader anti-establishment movement, with protesters accusing both the government and opposition parties of failing to address the country's political and economic challenges.

entry.title: Europe Today: EU Sports Commissioner on Euronews as biggest-ever World Cup kicks off
entry.content: The much anticipated FIFA World Cup kicks off today, bringing together some of the world's biggest footballers for a month of matches. We speak exclusively to Glenn Micallef, the EU Commissioner for Sports to hear the Brussels view on one of the most political World Cups to date.

entry.title: Why Europe's labour needs clash with its migration policy
entry.content: Europe faces a paradox: economies need workers while governments tighten migration rules. This tension grows as the EU’s Migration and Asylum Pact takes effect on 12 June, marking the bloc’s strictest migration policy in decades.

entry.title: Inside DATALAND: The world’s first AI art museum will let visitors see, hear and smell art
entry.content: Los Angeles is set to open DATALAND, the world's first museum dedicated to AI-generated art. Created by digital artist Refik Anadol, the immersive space combines AI and environmental data to create a multi-sensory experience.

entry.title: From Japan With Love: London exhibition explores how NIGO reshaped fashion, music and hype culture
entry.content: The exhibition, NIGO: From Japan With Love, brings together over 700 objects from his personal archive, charting his evolution from teenage Americana obsessive to global designer and artistic director of Kenzo.

entry.title: From Budapest’s famous baths to Alton Towers: The world’s most disappointing tourist attractions
entry.content: Bucket-list attractions don't always deliver. A study analysing almost 100,000 reviews of 200 major sights has revealed the destinations most likely to leave visitors underwhelmed, with complaints ranging from crowds and poor value for money to experiences that simply don't justify the hype.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 11th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 11th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: US and Iran trade strikes for second day, threatening return of all-out war
entry.content: The new US assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted global energy supplies and sent oil prices higher.

entry.title: World Cup: Has Trump already ruined the vibes? MEPs debate in The Ring
entry.content: The World Cup is on, the single biggest sport event of the year, followed by millions of people around the world. So far, the vibes are strangely subdued. Ticket sales and hotel bookings are below expectations and immigration issues are making headlines - especially in the US.

entry.title: Why are World Cup tickets so expensive?
entry.content: An expert says strong demand, dynamic pricing and a booming resale market are pushing World Cup ticket prices higher. As teams advance and knockout matchups become clearer, prices could continue to fluctuate based on fan interest and demand.

entry.title: Somalia's World Cup referee returns home to hero's welcome after US entry denial
entry.content: Somali referee Omar Artan was welcomed by hundreds of supporters in Mogadishu after being denied entry to the United States and removed from the 2026 World Cup officials list. Artan vowed to return for the next tournament.

entry.title: Infantino defends World Cup ticket prices, says fans 'should chill' about ref denied US entry
entry.content: The 2026 men's World Cup has been expanded to 48 teams and is the biggest and most ambitious in the event's history, with matches taking place in Mexico, Canada and the United States.

entry.title: 'Children are dying,' Cuba says as US blockade hampers delivery of UN aid
entry.content: The blockade, coupled with expanded US sanctions that punish companies doing business with the Cuban state, has intensified the worst economic and energy crisis on the island in over a generation.

entry.title: Trump says 'I love the inflation,' claims US taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil
entry.content: President Donald Trump welcomed new inflation data showing prices rising 4.2%, saying the numbers were "great." He argued higher energy costs linked to the Iran conflict were temporary and claimed US forces were moving millions of barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: ‘Amaterasu’ performance marks 160 years of Belgium-Japan relations in Brussels
entry.content: The Japanese performance Amaterasu was staged at Brussels Town Hall as part of celebrations marking the 160th anniversary of relations between Belgium and Japan. Organised by Be-Japon, the event showcased Japanese heritage through kimono fashion and brought together cultural enthusiasts.

entry.title: 'We have already given enough': Bulgaria's PM says weapons supplies to Ukraine will end
entry.content: Bulgaria, which is member of NATO and the European Union, has been providing military aid Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

entry.title: Long-term fertiliser solutions needed to avoid EU food shortages, EU Agriculture Commissioner says
entry.content: The Agriculture and Food Commissioner warned that, to avoid looming food shortages in the face of rising fertiliser costs, the bloc must secure sustainable fertiliser supplies and not just provide short-term financial support.

entry.title: 'Partners and friends’: Trade and defence top of agenda at EU-South Korea summit
entry.content: As security threats grow and geopolitics shift, the European Union and Republic of South Korea have celebrated a new Digital Trade Agreement at a summit in Brussels.

entry.title: UN atomic watchdog demands urgent cooperation from Iran and access to nuclear sites
entry.content: The resolution comes at a time of heightened tensions in the Middle East, with the US having launched airstrikes against Iran early on Wednesday and Tehran firing back at countries in the region.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 10th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 10th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Trust in France's institutions 'at stake' after girl's killing, Emmanuel Macron says
entry.content: The body of the girl, named as Lyhanna, was found last week after she went missing on 29 May in the southwestern town of Fleurance.

entry.title: Pakistan army helicopter crashes in Kashmir after technical fault, killing all on board
entry.content: Witnesses said that the helicopter crashed shortly after take-off from a helipad. Ambulances arrived at the scene and transported the victims to a nearby hospital.

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Disclosure Day' - Close Encounters Of The Fourth Kind?
entry.content: Steven Spielberg revisits his passion for interstellar visitors, with a government conspiracy begging to be blown wide open... NB: Spoiler-free review.

entry.title: Patrick Bruel scandal: French prosecutors seek charges and pre-trial detention over sex abuse claims
entry.content: After 48 hours in custody, the Nanterre prosecutor on Wednesday sought formal charges and pre-trial detention for the 67-year-old artist, accused of multiple sexual assaults he vehemently denies.

entry.title: Multiple attackers kill 12 people in late-night shooting in South Africa, police say
entry.content: Police said the motive was not yet known and no arrests had been made, but there were suspicions it was related to competition over illegal mining in the area.

entry.title: Deadly clashes in Kenya as protesters fight US Ebola quarantine facility
entry.content: Kenya saw fresh unrest on 9 June as protesters defied a court order and clashed with police near Laikipia Air Base over plans for a US funded Ebola quarantine facility, fuelling fears about the virus and prompting arrests.

entry.title: Hong Kong charges seven people and two firms over massive fire that killed 168
entry.content: The massive blaze, which engulfed seven of the eight high-rise apartment blocks at the Wang Fuk Court estate in November, prompted a months-long investigation into the cause.

entry.title: Kyiv hit Russian military plant using Ukrainian-made Flamingo missile, Zelenskyy says
entry.content: Volodymyr Zelenskyy vowed to continue Kyiv’s strikes deep inside Russia in response to attacks on Ukraine, but also a way of forcing the Kremlin into direct talks.

entry.title: Five EU countries push tougher safeguards for future members to avoid Orbán repeat
entry.content: Germany, France, the Netherlands, Belgium and Luxembourg want stronger safeguards to act faster against democratic and legal breaches in future EU members. The proposal reflects the lessons learned from the Orbán era.

entry.title: UK: Belfast violence leaves homes burnt and families displaced
entry.content: Belfast awoke to scenes of destruction on 10 June after anti immigration protests descended into violence across several parts of Northern Ireland, leaving homes damaged, vehicles burnt out and families displaced.

entry.title: Trump vows fresh attacks on Iran and accuses negotiators of 'playing us for suckers'
entry.content: The US launched air strikes against Iran early on Wednesday and Tehran fired back at countries in the region in escalating attacks that threatened to derail efforts to end the war.

entry.title: Julian Barnes quits fiction after winning 2026 Princess of Asturias for literature
entry.content: The British novelist said at a recent launch of his essay 'Farewells' at Barcelona's Caixafòrum that this would be his final novel, after turning 80 and spending over 45 years devoted to literature.

entry.title: 'Europe should evolve its carbon market, not dilute it,' investors say
entry.content: A group of investors managing €11.4 trillion in funds are advocating for complementary industrial policies such as grids, electrification and clean tech financing, reflecting a broader shift in European climate policy from setting targets toward managing industrial transformation.

entry.title: A turning point: Why Germany’s military capabilities are underestimated
entry.content: Many people believe that Germans are not prepared for defence. In a guest article for Euronews, military sociologist Dr Timo Graf uses recent survey data to show why this view underestimates German society’s capacity for defence.

entry.title: Subway station in Mexico City goes viral over chandeliers
entry.content: Decorative chandeliers installed at a busy Mexico City subway station ahead of the 2026 World Cup have sparked a wave of memes and mixed reactions from commuters.

entry.title: These are all the carriers banned from flying in Europe after EU Air Safety List update
entry.content: With the EU’s latest Air Safety List including Air Express Algeria among carriers banned from operating within European airspace, here’s what travellers need to know about how airlines end up on it.

entry.title: Thai woman in custody after US diplomat found dead in Myanmar, State Department says
entry.content: The Thai Embassy in Yangon and Thailand's Foreign Ministry refused to say whether they had provided consular support to the suspect or give any other information.

entry.title: One dead and two missing in missile strike on tanker near Oman
entry.content: The Palau-flagged Settebello transmitted a distress call on Wednesday reporting a missile strike on its engine room off Sohar in the Gulf of Oman, where local authorities are assisting with crew evacuation, the UK maritime agency reported.

entry.title: Philippines continues rescue effort after powerful Mindanao quake
entry.content: The Philippines continued rescue operations on 10 June after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Mindanao, killing at least 37 people, injuring nearly 500 and forcing more than 32,000 residents from their homes.

entry.title: Germany's most expensive hotels: luxury stays up to €53,000 a night
entry.content: In Germany's most exclusive hotels, a night costs up to 53,000 euros. These seven suites are setting new standards in comfort, service and prestige.

entry.title: ‘Europe cannot afford bureaucratic paralysis’: EU strikes defence simplification deal
entry.content: Facing Russian aggression and US retreat, the EU is planning to streamline permits, cut bureaucracy and make defence procurement and transfers across member states easier.

entry.title: Madrid’s Nobu Hotel to open in September with three-storey restaurant from famed Japanese chef
entry.content: Spain’s luxury hotel scene is set for a major addition this September with the grand opening of Nobu Hotel Madrid. Opening ahead of the Formula 1 weekend, the design-led stay promises skyline views, signature Chef Nobu Matsuhisa dishes and a prime location in the capital.

entry.title: Netherlands: Marktweg in The Hague becomes iconic orange street for World Cup fever
entry.content: Residents of Marktweg in The Hague have once again transformed their street into a sea of orange ahead of the FIFA World Cup, continuing a popular local tradition that has become a symbol of community spirit and support for the national team.

entry.title: Defying Russia: Poland and Germany plan massive offshore wind farms in the Baltic Sea
entry.content: Despite hybrid attacks from Russia, Poland is betting on offshore wind power from the Baltic Sea. At a forum in Berlin, one message was clear: the region could become Europe’s next major energy hub. Will Europe seize this opportunity?

entry.title: The world’s best hotel bars have just been named by Forbes – and 13 are in Europe
entry.content: From a bar in a former bank vault to a spot serving up caviar chicken nuggets, these hotel bars are a cut above the rest.

entry.title: Europe’s chaotic Entry/Exit System could take up to two years to stabilise, EU official warns
entry.content: Two months after Europe’s new biometric border checks launched, long queues and inconsistent procedures continue to frustrate travellers. Now, a Frontex official has warned that the troubled Entry/Exit System could take up to two years to fully stabilise.

entry.title: Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan receives warm welcome home after being denied entry to US
entry.content: Andrew Giuliani, who is leading the White House task force for the FIFA World Cup, has said Artan was denied entry to the US for a "very good reason," without offering further details.

entry.title: How AI and satellites can help protect our oceans | Euronews Tech Talks
entry.content: The European Digital Twin Ocean builds on artificial intelligence and satellite technology to create a virtual replica of the ocean for scientists and policymakers. How does it work?

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 10th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 10th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Spain: Pope Leo XIV surprises passengers with cockpit visit on Madrid-Barcelona flight
entry.content: Pope Leo XIV made a rare visit to the cockpit mid-flight before greeting passengers and crew during his flight from Madrid to Barcelona on 9 June, marking the second stage of his week-long visit to Spain.
